name: Thoracic Spine MRI Without Contrast
description: Standard thoracic spine MRI report template without contrast for evaluation of spinal cord and canal pathology

findings.technique: MRI of the thoracic spine was performed including T1-weighted, T2-weighted, and STIR sequences in sagittal and axial planes.
findings.comparison: No prior studies available for comparison.

sections.name: Vertebral Bodies
sections.content: The thoracic vertebral bodies from T1 through T12 demonstrate [normal height / no compression fracture / no bony lesion]. The bone marrow signal is [normal / no abnormal signal].

sections.name: Disc Levels
sections.content: At each disc level from T1-T2 through T11-T12, the intervertebral discs demonstrate [normal height / no bulge / no protrusion / no herniation]. No significant disc disease.

sections.name: Spinal Canal
sections.content: The spinal canal is [patent / adequate in diameter]. No significant canal stenosis. No mass lesion in the canal.

sections.name: Spinal Cord
sections.content: The thoracic spinal cord demonstrates [normal T2 signal / no cord compression / no cord edema / no myelomalacia]. The cord is [normal in caliber / no atrophy]. The conus medullaris is at the [T12 / L1] level.

sections.name: Neural Foramina
sections.content: The bilateral neural foramina are [patent / adequate in size / no foraminal narrowing]. No nerve root compression.

sections.name: Posterior Elements
sections.content: The posterior elements including the pedicles, laminae, facet joints, and spinous processes are [intact / no fracture / no lesion]. The ligamentum flavum is [not hypertrophied / normal].

sections.name: Paravertebral Soft Tissues
sections.content: The paravertebral soft tissues are [unremarkable / no abnormal signal / no mass]. No paraspinal abnormality.

sections.name: Epidural Space
sections.content: The epidural space is [normal / no mass / no abnormal enhancement]. No epidural fibrosis or lipomatosis.

impression:
1. Normal thoracic spine MRI.
2. No disc herniation, spinal canal stenosis, or cord abnormality identified.
3. The conus medullaris is at normal T12/L1 level.
author: RadReport Templates
